key_points: A polynomial is an algebraic expression containing variables with non-negative integer exponents
The degree of a polynomial is the greatest exponent of the variable
Polynomials can be classified as constant, linear, quadratic, cubic or higher degree
Zeroes of a polynomial are the values of the variable that make the polynomial equal to zero
Factor theorem helps determine factors of polynomials
Factorisation simplifies polynomial expressions into products of simpler expressions
Algebraic identities allow expansion and simplification of expressions
Polynomial concepts are useful in solving equations and mathematical modeling
exam_tips: Check the structure of the polynomial before attempting factorisation
Use algebraic identities to expand or simplify expressions quickly
Practice solving problems involving polynomial evaluation
Understand the relationship between zeroes and factors of polynomials
Always verify your factorisation by multiplying the factors
